data: S17-26-OL WS2026 International Online Training Competition

Aspect
Type
M = Meas
J = Judg

Extra Aspect Description (Meas or Judg)
OR
Judgement Score Description (Judg only)
Code is entirely unorganized, placed in a single file with no logical structure.
Code has basic organization but lacks clear component/module separation.
Code is well-organized with logical file structure, separate components, and clear naming conventions.
Code is excellently organized with highly modular structure, efficient state management, strict adherence to framework/vanilla best practices, and is highly maintainable.
Test: Load the application. Verify the view contains the game title, instructions (movement and pause), and the 'Start Learning Path' button.
Test: Intentionally die in the game. Verify the Game Over screen displays the final score (Certificates Earned) and a button to restart.
Test: Die by hitting a wall, check that the text explicitly says 'Burnout'. Restart and die by hitting the tail, check that the text explicitly says 'Spaghetti Code'.
Test: Look at the top bar during gameplay. Verify all three statistics are visible: Certificates Earned, Developer Level, and Best Run.
Developer Level calculation explained in UI
Test: Look at the HUD. Verify there is visible text or a tooltip explaining the formula for how the Developer Level is calculated.
Test: Resize the browser window width down to 375px. Verify that the layout adapts without horizontal scrolling and the game board remains visually intact.
Design ignores the SkillShare Academy theme, using generic or default styling with poor contrast.
Design applies basic theme colors but lacks polish, hierarchy, or visual cohesion.
Design clearly follows the SkillShare Academy visual direction (dark UI, emerald accents) with good contrast and clear layout.
Exceptional UI/UX implementation that perfectly captures the brand guidelines, featuring highly polished typography, excellent contrast, and an engaging layout.
Game event CSS animation or transition implemented
Test: Play the game. Verify that at least ONE CSS animation or transition occurs (e.g., pulse on collection, shake on game over, fade-in on a menu).
Test: Inspect the game board using DevTools or count manually. Verify it is a finite grid of exactly 20x20 cells using either CSS Grid or Canvas.
Test: Start the game and grow the snake. Verify the Student (head), Knowledge Base (body), and IT Skill (food) are clearly distinguishable from one another.
Test: Click 'Start Learning Path'. Verify the Student automatically moves one cell per tick without holding any keyboard input.
Test: Play the game using physical Arrow Keys and WASD keys. Verify both control schemes correctly change the Student's direction of movement.
Test: While moving RIGHT, immediately press LEFT. Verify the input is completely ignored and the snake does NOT instantly reverse into itself.
Test: While moving, press Space to verify the game loop stops completely. Press Space again to verify it resumes.
Test: Press Space to pause. Verify a modal or overlay appears explicitly indicating the game is paused.
Test: Navigate the Student directly into the outer boundary of the grid. Verify the game ends immediately.
Test: Grow a tail. Drive the Student into its own tail. Verify the game ends immediately.
Test: Trigger a Game Over and click 'Restart'. Verify Certificates reset to 0, Developer Level resets, the Knowledge Base shrinks back, and the game loop starts over.
Test: Navigate into an IT Skill. Verify Certificates Earned increases by 1 AND the snake body visibly grows by 1 segment.
Test: Collect an IT Skill. Verify a new skill instantly appears on the board and does NOT overlap with the Student or the existing tail.
Test: Collect IT Skills. Verify level math matches: 0-1 certificates = Level 1. Upon collecting the 2nd certificate, Level jumps to 2. Upon 4th, jumps to 3.
Test: Score exactly 3 points. Trigger Game Over. Open DevTools -> Application -> Local Storage. Verify a key storing the high score is updated.
Test: Ensure a Best Run > 0 is saved. Refresh the browser page. Verify the 'Best Run' in the HUD successfully loads and displays the saved value.
